question.text: An NRI invests in an equity mutual fund in India. Upon redemption after 15 months with a long-term capital gain, what is the TDS obligation of the AMC?

answer.text: TDS at 12.5% on LTCG exceeding Rs. 1.25 lakh plus applicable surcharge and cess
No TDS for NRIs as they file returns in their country of residence
TDS at 10% as per the old LTCG rate for NRIs
TDS at 30% flat rate on all NRI mutual fund redemptions

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** For NRI investors, TDS is mandatory on mutual fund redemption proceeds. For equity LTCG (holding >12 months), TDS is deducted at 12.5% on gains exceeding Rs. 1.25 lakh (Section 112A rate), plus applicable surcharge and health & education cess. Unlike resident investors where no TDS applies on capital gains, AMCs must deduct TDS for NRIs.
**The Trap:** Option B is completely wrong - TDS is mandatory for NRIs. Option C uses old 10% rate. Option D uses an incorrect flat 30% rate. NRI TDS follows the actual tax rate applicable to the type of gain.
**Cross-Exam Context:** NRI taxation on MFs differs primarily in the TDS mechanism. For STCG (equity), TDS is at 20%. For debt fund gains, TDS is at slab rates. NRIs can claim DTAA benefits if applicable.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** NRI clients should be informed about TDS on redemptions upfront. They can claim credit for TDS in their Indian tax return or claim refund if excess TDS is deducted. DTAA provisions may provide relief.
